channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: AmplifyChange Strengthening Grants 2026: Up to £200,000 Available for SRHR Advocacy Projects in Eligible Countries
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Civil society organisations working to advance sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRHR) now have an exciting opportunity to secure major funding through the 2026 AmplifyChange Strengthening Grants programme. Applications are officially open from 21 May to 16 June 2026, offering grants between £100,000 and £200,000 for projects lasting 24 to 36 months. This funding […]

The post AmplifyChange Strengthening Grants 2026: Up to £200,000 Available for SRHR Advocacy Projects in Eligible Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Civil society organisations working to advance sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRHR) now have an exciting opportunity to secure major funding through the 2026 AmplifyChange Strengthening Grants programme. Applications are officially open from 21 May to 16 June 2026, offering grants between £100,000 and £200,000 for projects lasting 24 to 36 months.

This funding opportunity is designed to support small to medium-sized civil society organisations seeking to strengthen their advocacy work, expand organisational capacity, and drive sustainable change in SRHR policies, services, and social norms.

What Are the AmplifyChange Strengthening Grants?

The AmplifyChange Strengthening Grants support advocacy initiatives focused on improving access to sexual and reproductive health and rights. A unique feature of this funding is that up to 40% of the grant can be used for organisational development and strengthening institutional effectiveness.

The programme aims to empower organisations that are working to influence policies, improve services, reduce stigma, and promote inclusive SRHR systems, particularly for marginalised and underserved populations.

Funding Details

Grant Amount

  • £100,000 – £200,000

Project Duration

  • 2 to 3 years (24–36 months)

Application Window

  • Opens: 21 May 2026
  • Deadline: 16 June 2026 by 17:00 UK Time

Registration Deadline

Applicants must register on the Grants Portal by:

  • 5 June 2026 at 12:00 UK Time

Due to high demand, organisations are encouraged to register early to avoid delays.

Key Priority Areas

AmplifyChange is looking to fund projects that align with one or more of its core SRHR priority themes:

  • Violence
  • Abortion
  • Inclusion
  • SRHR Education
  • Access to SRH Services

Projects can also address intersections between multiple themes.

In response to global funding trends, at least 30% of available funding will specifically support applications focused on:

  • Governments ensuring funding for SRHR

Recognising that some legally marginalised groups may face challenges engaging directly in this area, organisations are also encouraged to submit proposals under other relevant outcome areas aligned with their expertise and operating context.

Outcome Areas

Applications should contribute to one or more of the following outcomes:

  • Increased awareness of SRHR as human rights
  • Policy and legal reforms improving SRHR access
  • Improved access to quality sexual and reproductive health services
  • More inclusive social norms and reduced stigma and discrimination
  • Governments ensuring sustainable funding for SRHR

Cross-Cutting Themes

AmplifyChange also welcomes projects integrating broader issues connected to SRHR and wellbeing, including:

  • Climate Justice
  • Mental Health
  • Pleasure and Bodily Autonomy

Who Can Apply?

To be eligible, organisations must:

  • Be registered and implementing projects in an eligible country
  • Have a functioning organisational bank account
  • Be a small to medium-sized organisation
  • Have an average annual expenditure between £150,000 and £350,000 over the past two years
  • Provide financial evidence such as audited reports or profit and loss statements

Only one application per organisation will be accepted during this funding round.

Eligible Countries

Projects must be implemented in eligible countries across Africa and Asia, including:

Angola, Bangladesh, Benin, Bhutan, Botswana, Burkina Faso, Burundi, Cameroon, Cape Verde, Central African Republic, Chad, Comoros, Côte d’Ivoire, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Djibouti, Equatorial Guinea, Eritrea, Eswatini, Ethiopia, Gabon, The Gambia, Ghana, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, India, Kenya, Lesotho, Liberia, Madagascar, Malawi, Maldives, Mali, Mauritania, Mauritius, Mozambique, Myanmar, Namibia, Nepal, Niger, Nigeria, Pakistan, Republic of Congo, Rwanda, São Tomé and Príncipe, Senegal, Sierra Leone, Somalia, South Africa, South Sudan, Sri Lanka, Sudan, Tanzania, Togo, Uganda, Zambia, and Zimbabwe.

What Type of Projects Are Encouraged?

AmplifyChange is particularly interested in projects that:

  • Strengthen organisational capacity and sustainability
  • Advocate for increased government funding for SRHR
  • Address discriminatory social norms and unequal power dynamics
  • Scale successful advocacy models
  • Support innovative approaches with proven early success
  • Empower marginalised communities to claim their rights
  • Influence laws, policies, and implementation systems
  • Build coalitions and collective action for long-term change

Focus on Marginalised Communities

The fund strongly prioritises organisations working with populations facing restricted access to SRHR, including:

  • Young people
  • Refugees
  • People with disabilities
  • Rural populations
  • Urban poor communities
  • Ethnic minorities
  • Sexual minorities

Webinar Opportunities for Applicants

Interested applicants can join free webinars to better understand the application process and requirements.

Webinar Dates

  • About the Grant: 21 May 2026 at 10:00 UK Time
  • Monitoring & Evaluation Requirements: 26 May 2026 at 10:00 UK Time

Why This Opportunity Matters

As global conversations around gender equality, reproductive justice, youth empowerment, and inclusive healthcare continue to evolve, funding opportunities like the AmplifyChange Strengthening Grants provide critical support for grassroots and civil society organisations leading transformative advocacy efforts.

For organisations committed to advancing SRHR and strengthening community-driven change, this funding round presents an important opportunity to scale impact, strengthen systems, and contribute to lasting policy and social transformation.

How to Apply

Interested organisations should begin preparing early, review the eligibility criteria carefully, and complete Grants Portal registration before the registration deadline.

Apply for the AmplifyChange Strengthening Grant

Are You an Existing User?

Login Here: https://amplifychange.fluxx.io/portal

Are You a New Applicant?

Register Here: https://amplifychange.fluxx.io/user_sessions/new?lang=en

FAQs

If you are unsure whether your organisation meets AmplifyChange’s eligibility criteria, be sure to review the Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) for detailed clarification on eligibility, funding requirements, and the application process.

Read More: https://amplifychange.org/faqs/

Application Guidance

Interested applicants are strongly encouraged to review the official application guidance to better understand what AmplifyChange is looking for, how applications are assessed, and tips for preparing a strong proposal.

Read More: https://amplifychange.org/application-guidance/

For more opportunities for youth-led organisations, NGOs, civil society groups, scholarships, fellowships, grants, jobs, and leadership programmes worldwide, keep visiting OFY and stay connected for the latest updates.

The post AmplifyChange Strengthening Grants 2026: Up to £200,000 Available for SRHR Advocacy Projects in Eligible Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Commonwealth Students’ Association Opens Applications for Campus Ambassadors Across the Commonwealth 2026
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The Commonwealth Students’ Association has officially launched applications for its highly anticipated 2026 Campus Ambassador Programme, inviting passionate and impact-driven students from tertiary institutions across Commonwealth nations to become representatives of student voices within their campuses and communities. This prestigious opportunity is designed to strengthen grassroots student engagement, leadership development, advocacy participation, and institutional collaboration […]

The post Commonwealth Students’ Association Opens Applications for Campus Ambassadors Across the Commonwealth 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Commonwealth Students’ Association has officially launched applications for its highly anticipated 2026 Campus Ambassador Programme, inviting passionate and impact-driven students from tertiary institutions across Commonwealth nations to become representatives of student voices within their campuses and communities.

This prestigious opportunity is designed to strengthen grassroots student engagement, leadership development, advocacy participation, and institutional collaboration throughout the Commonwealth. Through this initiative, selected ambassadors will serve as a direct link between the Commonwealth Students’ Association (CSA) and students within their universities or colleges, helping to promote meaningful participation in student-centered programmes and campaigns.

The CSA Campus Ambassador Programme offers students an opportunity to develop leadership skills, build international networks, participate in advocacy initiatives, and contribute to shaping the future of student representation across Commonwealth countries.

About the Commonwealth Students’ Association Campus Ambassador Programme

The CSA Campus Ambassador Programme is aimed at empowering students who are passionate about leadership, student engagement, advocacy, and community development.

Selected ambassadors will become official student representatives of the Commonwealth Students’ Association within their institutions. Ambassadors will support the implementation of CSA initiatives on campus while mobilising fellow students to participate in activities, campaigns, and programmes that promote student empowerment and collaboration.

The initiative also seeks to build stronger connections between student communities across different Commonwealth countries, encouraging knowledge sharing, dialogue, and youth participation in global conversations.

Responsibilities of CSA Campus Ambassadors

Students selected for the programme will play an active role in promoting the vision and activities of the Commonwealth Students’ Association within their institutions.

Key responsibilities include:

  1. Promoting CSA programmes, initiatives, and campaigns within campus communities.
  2. Mobilising students to participate in leadership and advocacy activities.
  3. Supporting institutional engagement and grassroots mobilisation.
  4. Encouraging student participation in research and policy conversations.
  5. Acting as a communication bridge between CSA and students on campus.
  6. Assisting with awareness campaigns and student-focused events.
  7. Strengthening student representation and advocacy efforts within institutions.

The role provides students with an opportunity to gain practical leadership experience while contributing meaningfully to their academic and social communities.

Who Can Apply?

The Commonwealth Students’ Association welcomes applications from students who meet the following eligibility criteria:

  • Applicants must currently be enrolled in a recognised tertiary institution within a Commonwealth country.
  • Applicants must be at least 18 years old.
  • Applicants should be in good academic standing.
  • Applicants must be able to dedicate a minimum of five hours per week to CSA activities.
  • Applicants should demonstrate enthusiasm for advocacy, grassroots mobilisation, and student engagement.
  • Applicants should possess strong interest in leadership and community impact.

Importantly, prior student leadership experience is considered an advantage but is not mandatory. This means emerging student leaders with passion, commitment, and dedication are strongly encouraged to apply.

Benefits of Becoming a CSA Campus Ambassador

The programme provides ambassadors with access to a range of personal, professional, and leadership development opportunities.

Benefits include:

1. Leadership Development Opportunities

Ambassadors will gain practical leadership experience through campus engagement activities, advocacy initiatives, and programme coordination.

2. Capacity Building and Mentorship

Participants will receive mentorship opportunities and access to learning experiences aimed at strengthening communication, leadership, and organisational skills.

3. International Networking

The programme connects ambassadors with students, youth leaders, and institutions across Commonwealth countries, creating opportunities for collaboration and global networking.

4. Participation in CSA Initiatives

Campus ambassadors will have opportunities to actively participate in various CSA programmes, campaigns, and advocacy activities.

5. Recognition and Certificates

Outstanding ambassadors may receive Certificates of Service and recommendation letters in recognition of their commitment and contributions.

Duration of Appointment

The CSA Campus Ambassador appointment will run for an initial one-year term. However, ambassadors who demonstrate exceptional commitment and performance may have opportunities for renewal.

Is the Programme Paid?

The Commonwealth Students’ Association has clarified that the Campus Ambassador role is voluntary and non-remunerated. Despite this, the programme offers significant value for students seeking to build leadership experience, expand professional networks, and contribute to international student advocacy initiatives.

Application Deadline

Interested applicants must submit their applications before:

15 June 2026 (11:59 PM GMT)

Students are encouraged to apply early to avoid last-minute issues during submission.

Why Students Should Apply

The CSA Campus Ambassador Programme represents a unique opportunity for students to become active contributors to student leadership and advocacy efforts within the Commonwealth.

Beyond campus representation, ambassadors gain exposure to international networks, leadership development opportunities, and meaningful engagement in conversations that shape the future of education and student empowerment.

Students who are passionate about leadership, youth development, advocacy, and community engagement are strongly encouraged to seize this opportunity and become part of a growing network of changemakers across the Commonwealth.

Learn more and apply

Interested students can apply through the official Commonwealth Students’ Association application portal HERE.

Click HERE to learn more about the Commonwealth Students’ Association for Campus Ambassadors .

Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here.

The post Commonwealth Students’ Association Opens Applications for Campus Ambassadors Across the Commonwealth 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Feminist Investigative Journalism Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded SOAS MA Global Data Journalism Online Opportunity by IJSC
item.dc:creator: Hildah W. Nakafu
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Feminist Investigative Journalism Scholarship offered through the Institute for Journalism and Social Change (IJSC) in partnership with the SOAS MA Global Data Journalism Online programme. This scholarship is designed for aspiring journalists, researchers, activists, storytellers, and social justice advocates who want to use investigative journalism and data-driven reporting to […]

The post Feminist Investigative Journalism Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded SOAS MA Global Data Journalism Online Opportunity by IJSC appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Feminist Investigative Journalism Scholarship offered through the Institute for Journalism and Social Change (IJSC) in partnership with the SOAS MA Global Data Journalism Online programme.

This scholarship is designed for aspiring journalists, researchers, activists, storytellers, and social justice advocates who want to use investigative journalism and data-driven reporting to expose injustice, challenge harmful systems, and create real-world impact. It is especially focused on journalism that advances gender justice, protects women’s rights, defends LGBTQI rights, and amplifies voices from communities that have historically been underrepresented in mainstream media.

The deadline to apply is 6pm GMT on 31st May 2026.

About the Feminist Investigative Journalism Scholarship

The Feminist Investigative Journalism Scholarship is an opportunity for students who want to study the SOAS MA Global Data Journalism Online programme while developing practical skills in investigative journalism, data journalism, feminist reporting, and cross-border storytelling.

The scholarship is offered in partnership with the Institute for Journalism and Social Change, an organization that brings together journalists, activists, researchers, and social justice advocates to work on powerful investigations and collaborative projects.

The scholarship is not only about funding a degree. It is also about supporting people who want to use journalism as a tool for justice. Successful applicants will gain access to training, mentorship, networking opportunities, and practical publishing experience.

This makes the scholarship especially valuable for applicants who want to build careers in:

  • Investigative journalism
  • Data journalism
  • Global journalism
  • Human rights reporting
  • Gender justice advocacy
  • NGO communications
  • Research and policy advocacy
  • Social justice media
  • Cross-border investigative projects

Scholarship Value and Benefits

The Feminist Investigative Journalism Scholarship offers significant academic, professional, and practical benefits.

Successful scholarship recipients will receive:

1. Full Tuition Fee Coverage

The scholarship covers 100% of tuition fees for the SOAS MA Global Data Journalism Online programme.

The tuition fee value is £12,000.

This means selected students will be able to pursue the programme without paying tuition fees, reducing one of the biggest barriers to postgraduate study.

2. Access to IJSC Networking and Training Events

Scholarship recipients will have opportunities to participate in IJSC networking and training events.

These events can help students connect with:

  • Experienced journalists
  • Investigative reporters
  • Researchers
  • Activists
  • Editors
  • Media professionals
  • Social justice organizations
  • Potential collaborators

For students who want to build careers in journalism or advocacy, these networks can be extremely valuable.

3. Mentorship and Career Advice

Recipients will also receive mentorship and career guidance from IJSC associates and advisors.

This mentorship can help students develop:

  • Investigative story ideas
  • Data journalism skills
  • Reporting strategies
  • Ethical journalism practices
  • Career direction
  • Publication opportunities
  • Confidence in professional storytelling

Mentorship is especially important in investigative journalism because strong investigations require not only technical skills but also editorial judgment, ethical awareness, persistence, and support.

4. Practical Projects for Publication

Scholarship recipients will have the opportunity to work on practical projects for publication.

This is one of the strongest parts of the scholarship because it allows students to move beyond theory and into real-world journalism practice.

Previous IJSC investigations have received global media attention, including coverage by major outlets such as:

  • Al Jazeera
  • The Guardian
  • Mail & Guardian

These investigations have also led to real-world impact. For example, in 2023, Norway, Denmark, and Sweden reportedly changed funding practices after IJSC revealed their support, through an intermediary, for anti-LGBT groups in Uganda, despite the donors’ rights commitments.

This shows that IJSC’s work is not only academic or theoretical. It can influence public debate, institutional accountability, donor practices, and human rights outcomes.

About the SOAS MA Global Data Journalism Online Programme

The SOAS MA Global Data Journalism Online programme is developed and delivered in partnership with the Institute for Journalism and Social Change.

The programme focuses on journalism that is global, data-driven, socially aware, and rooted in justice. It is especially suitable for students who want to understand how journalism can expose injustice, challenge dominant narratives, and support marginalized communities.

Because the programme is online, students can study remotely from wherever they are based. This makes it accessible to applicants who may not be able to relocate for postgraduate study.

Key Programme Information

Here are the main details applicants should know:

Start Date

The programme starts in September 2026.

Duration

Students can study either:

  1. Full-time for 1 year, or
  2. Part-time for 2 years

This flexibility makes the programme suitable for applicants who may be balancing study with work, family responsibilities, activism, freelance journalism, or other commitments.

Location

The programme is delivered online.

This scholarship opportunity is specifically for students who will study remotely.

Students will be able to work on practical cross-border projects while earning their degree from wherever they are based.

Number of Scholarships Available

There are two scholarships available.

Because only two scholarships are available, applicants should take time to prepare a strong, thoughtful, and well-written application.

Why This Scholarship Matters

This scholarship matters because journalism is one of the most powerful tools for exposing injustice and holding institutions accountable.

However, many communities most affected by injustice are often underrepresented in the media. Their stories may be ignored, misrepresented, or told without context. This scholarship seeks to support people who can bring lived experience, ethical reporting, and feminist analysis into investigative journalism.

The opportunity is especially important for applicants interested in stories related to:

  • Women’s rights
  • LGBTQI rights
  • Gender-based injustice
  • Social inequality
  • Global South perspectives
  • Donor accountability
  • Human rights
  • Media representation
  • Data-driven investigations
  • Social justice reporting

Why You Should Apply

There are several strong reasons to apply for the Feminist Investigative Journalism Scholarship.

1. You Will Learn Specialized Investigative and Data Journalism Skills

The programme helps students learn how to tell powerful, evidence-based stories.

You will learn how to use data, research, investigation, and storytelling to expose issues affecting women, LGBTQI communities, and other marginalized groups around the world.

This is important because many modern investigations require more than interviews and traditional reporting. Journalists increasingly need to work with:

  • Data sets
  • Documents
  • Cross-border evidence
  • Public records
  • Digital research tools
  • Collaborative reporting methods
  • Human rights frameworks

2. You Will Study Journalism Through Feminist and Decolonized Approaches

The curriculum emphasizes inclusive and justice-oriented perspectives.

This means the programme does not treat journalism as neutral in a shallow way. Instead, it recognizes that power, history, gender, race, geography, and colonial legacies shape how stories are told and whose voices are heard.

A feminist and decolonized approach to journalism asks important questions such as:

  • Who is being represented?
  • Who is being ignored?
  • Who benefits from the current narrative?
  • Whose evidence is treated as credible?
  • How can journalism avoid reproducing harm?
  • How can stories be told with dignity and context?

3. You Can Study Online From Wherever You Are

The programme is fully online, allowing students to study remotely.

This is especially useful for applicants who cannot relocate due to cost, visa barriers, employment, family responsibilities, safety concerns, or community commitments.

The online format makes the scholarship more accessible while still allowing students to participate in meaningful cross-border journalism projects.

4. You Can Turn Lived Experience Into Impactful Journalism

This scholarship specifically values applicants who want to use their lived experience to inform their journalism.

That does not mean applicants must only report on their personal experiences. Rather, it means the scholarship recognizes that people who have experienced or closely witnessed injustice can bring important insight, sensitivity, and urgency to their reporting.

Lived experience can help journalists ask better questions, notice overlooked patterns, challenge stereotypes, and tell stories with greater depth.

5. The Programme Can Support Multiple Career Paths

The SOAS MA Global Data Journalism Online programme can prepare students for careers in several fields, including:

  • Global journalism
  • Investigative journalism
  • Data journalism
  • NGO work
  • Human rights advocacy
  • Policy research
  • Campaign communications
  • Social justice storytelling
  • Media development
  • Research-based advocacy

This makes the scholarship valuable not only for people who want to work in newsrooms, but also for those who want to use journalism skills in civil society, advocacy, research, or international development.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet the academic and programme requirements.

To be eligible, applicants should have:

  • A minimum 2:2 UK undergraduate degree, or
  • An international equivalent qualification

Scholarship applicants must also separately apply to the SOAS MA Global Data Journalism Online programme.

Scholarship awards are conditional on gaining entry to the programme. This means that even if an applicant is selected for the scholarship, they must also be accepted into the MA programme before the scholarship can be confirmed.

How to Apply

To apply for the Feminist Investigative Journalism Scholarship, applicants must complete the online application form.

The application form will ask applicants to:

  1. Paste in their CV.
  2. Answer three written questions.

Each answer should be between 300 and 400 words.

Application Questions

Applicants will be asked to respond to three questions.

1. How would this scholarship help you apply your lived experience to impactful journalism?

This question asks applicants to explain the connection between their background, lived experience, and their journalism goals.

A strong answer should show:

  • What experiences have shaped your perspective
  • Why those experiences matter for journalism
  • How you want to use your insight responsibly
  • What kind of impact you hope to create
  • Why this scholarship is important for your journey

2. What is your specific interest in investigative and data journalism for gender justice?

This question focuses on your motivation and area of interest.

A strong answer may discuss:

  • Why investigative journalism matters to you
  • Why data journalism is important for accountability
  • What gender justice issues you care about
  • How evidence-based reporting can expose injustice
  • What kinds of stories you want to investigate

3. Give an example of a story that could have been told better with a feminist approach, and explain why.

This question tests your ability to think critically about media coverage.

A strong answer should identify a story and explain:

  • How it was originally reported
  • What was missing from the coverage
  • Whose voices were ignored
  • What assumptions shaped the story
  • How a feminist approach would have improved it
  • Why better reporting would have mattered

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply is 6pm GMT on 31st May 2026.

Applicants should prepare their materials early, especially because the application requires a CV and three written responses of 300 to 400 words each.

Since there are only two scholarships available, applicants should avoid submitting rushed answers. Strong applications should be clear, specific, personal, and connected to the scholarship’s purpose.

Final Thoughts

The Feminist Investigative Journalism Scholarship is a powerful opportunity for people who want to use journalism to challenge injustice and create social change.

With full tuition coverage worth £12,000, mentorship, training, networking, and opportunities to work on practical projects for publication, this scholarship offers more than financial support. It offers a pathway into meaningful, justice-focused journalism.

For applicants passionate about gender justice, LGBTQI rights, Global South perspectives, data journalism, and investigative reporting, this opportunity could be a major step toward building a career that combines storytelling, evidence, activism, and public accountability.

Applications close at 6pm GMT on 31st May 2026.

APPLY HERE.

For more information about this opportunity click here.

Discover more Scholarship opportunities on OFY here.

The post Feminist Investigative Journalism Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded SOAS MA Global Data Journalism Online Opportunity by IJSC appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Malmö Residency 2026: A Month‑Long Creative Residency in Sweden’s Most Experimental City
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The Malmö Residency 2026 invites innovators, creators, researchers, entrepreneurs, and explorers from around the world to spend one month in Malmö—Sweden’s vibrant, youthful, and forward‑thinking coastal city. This city‑wide residency provides time, space, and community support to help you start, develop, or complete a project, idea, or exploration. Applications are open until June 3, 2026. […]

The post Malmö Residency 2026: A Month‑Long Creative Residency in Sweden’s Most Experimental City appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Malmö Residency 2026 invites innovators, creators, researchers, entrepreneurs, and explorers from around the world to spend one month in Malmö—Sweden’s vibrant, youthful, and forward‑thinking coastal city. This city‑wide residency provides time, space, and community support to help you start, develop, or complete a project, idea, or exploration.

Applications are open until June 3, 2026. This opportunity includes travel, accommodation, workspaces, community access, and deep immersion into Malmö’s creative and business ecosystems.

About the Malmö Residency

The Malmö business community collectively hosts this residency, offering participants a unique environment to focus on their ideas while connecting with local innovators. As stated in the document:

“We – the business community of Malmö – invite you to spend time in the not-so-royal third-biggest city in Sweden.”

The residency takes place in September/October 2026, with a mandatory stay between September 9 and September 20.

Only five exclusive spots are available for the 2026 cohort.

Priority Themes for 2026

For this third cohort, Malmö is especially interested in applicants excited by the following themes:

  • Green Transition – Developing regenerative futures
  • AI and Emerging Technologies – Elevating society, business, and creative work
  • Food Innovation – Revolutionizing food production within city limits
  • Cultural & Creative Industries – Games, art, dance, music, and more
  • Wise & Digital Cities – Making cities transparent, democratic, and fun

These themes reflect Malmö’s identity as a hub of experimentation and cross‑disciplinary collaboration.

What’s Included in the Residency

Participants receive a comprehensive package of support and resources, including:

  • Travel to and from Malmö (preferably by train)
  • One month of accommodation
  • A bike for local mobility
  • Access to workspaces across the city
  • Advice from a broad community of experts
  • Access to event venues, equipment, and production guidance
  • Equipment and books to borrow
  • Material libraries
  • Industry‑grade testbeds
  • Introductions to Malmö’s startup and game development communities
  • Exhibitions at museums and art spaces
  • Outdoor experiences, including beaches and allemansrätten
  • Access to Malmö’s top‑ranked food and beverage scene

The document emphasizes:

“No strings attached really. We are a curious group and know that we benefit from an influx of open-minded people.”

Why Malmö?

Malmö is a coastal, diverse, and youthful city known for its openness and experimental culture. With 366,644 inhabitants, it is Sweden’s third‑largest and fastest‑growing major city. Nearly half the population is under 35, and 187 nationalities are represented.

Key highlights:

  • A strong pay‑it‑forward culture
  • 39,000 companies, mostly small and medium‑sized
  • A thriving arts, design, and food scene
  • Close proximity to Copenhagen Airport (20 minutes)
  • 5 hours by train from Hamburg
  • A balance of small‑city ease and big‑city ambition

The Region and Academic Ecosystem

The residency is embedded in the Öresund region—home to 4.2 million people and the largest labor market in the Nordics.

Nearby institutions include:

  • Malmö University – Strong in Interaction Design, Urban Studies, IoT, Data Society
  • Lund University – One of the oldest universities in the world
  • Swedish University of Agricultural Sciences (Alnarp) – Leading in food, agriculture, and landscape architecture
  • World Maritime University – Global leader in maritime and ocean research

This academic richness provides fertile ground for collaboration and inspiration.

Events During the Residency

Participants will be in Malmö during major international events, including:

  • ECCV 2026 – European Conference on Computer Vision (Sept 8–12)
  • The Drop – (Sept 15–16)
  • EUMAS 2026 – (Sept 21–25)

These events offer cutting‑edge knowledge and networking opportunities.

Workspaces Available

Participants will have access to Malmö’s most dynamic co‑working and innovation hubs:

  • STPLN
  • Media Evolution
  • Minc
  • Djäkne
  • The Ground
  • Form/Design Center

Visa Information

The residency cannot assist with visa applications. Applicants from visa‑required countries must secure their own documentation.

How to Apply

Deadline: June 3, 2026

Applications are open to global applicants who can commit to the mandatory residency dates.

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here.

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Malmö Residency 2026: A Month‑Long Creative Residency in Sweden’s Most Experimental City appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships 2026/2027: Fully Funded PhD Scholarship for At-Risk Scholars
item.dc:creator: Hildah W. Nakafu
item.description:
Loughborough University is offering the Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships for students beginning their doctoral studies in October 2026, February 2027, or July 2027. This scholarship has been created to support individuals whose education, safety, and life opportunities have been disrupted by conflict, persecution, displacement, or serious threats in their home countries. The Sanctuary Scholarship […]

The post Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships 2026/2027: Fully Funded PhD Scholarship for At-Risk Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Loughborough University is offering the Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships for students beginning their doctoral studies in October 2026, February 2027, or July 2027. This scholarship has been created to support individuals whose education, safety, and life opportunities have been disrupted by conflict, persecution, displacement, or serious threats in their home countries.

The Sanctuary Scholarship scheme reflects Loughborough University’s commitment to widening access to higher education for people who may otherwise be unable to pursue advanced study. For many scholars facing danger, forced displacement, or political and social instability, continuing education can become extremely difficult or impossible without targeted institutional support. This scholarship is designed to remove major financial barriers and help eligible candidates pursue a PhD in a safe and supportive academic environment.

The scholarship covers 100% of tuition fees and provides a stipend of £21,805 per year, set at the recommended UKRI rate, for up to three years of full-time PhD study. The stipend is also subject to annual uplifts. Part-time PhD study in the UK may also be considered under specific conditions.

The application deadline is Monday 22 June 2026.

About the Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships

The Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships are intended for individuals who can be described as at-risk scholars. These are people whose lives, education, or future opportunities have been seriously affected by dangerous or unstable circumstances such as conflict, persecution, war, displacement, or threats to personal safety.

The scheme exists because Loughborough University recognises that talent and academic potential are not always matched by equal access to opportunity. Some highly capable students may be unable to progress to doctoral study because they have been forced to leave their home, live in unsafe conditions, or face persecution if they remain in or return to their country.

Through this scholarship, the University aims to provide access to advanced research study for people who need meaningful institutional support to continue their academic journey.

Scholarship Benefits

Successful applicants for the Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships will receive a generous support package for doctoral study.

The scholarship provides:

  1. Full tuition fee coverage

    The scholarship covers 100% of tuition fees for the duration of the award. This removes one of the biggest financial barriers to undertaking a PhD at a UK university.

  2. Annual stipend

    Scholars will receive a stipend set at the recommended UKRI rate of £21,805 per year.

  3. Three years of support for full-time PhD study

    The award is available for a period of three years for full-time PhD study.

  4. Annual stipend uplifts

    The stipend is subject to annual uplifts, meaning the amount may increase in line with relevant updates.

  5. Part-time study may be considered

    Applicants who wish to study part-time in the UK may also be considered, but specific conditions apply.

  6. University-funded scholarship

    The scholarships are funded through Loughborough University funds.

What the Scholarship Does Not Cover

Although this is a highly supportive scholarship, applicants should note that it does not cover every possible cost connected to study in the UK.

The scholarship does not cover:

  • Travel costs
  • Visa application costs
  • Other related relocation costs, where required

Students are expected to fund their own travel and visa costs through other sources.

This is an important point for applicants to consider before applying, especially those who are currently outside the UK and may need to relocate in order to begin their PhD studies.

Entry Dates for the Scholarship

The Sanctuary PhD Scholarships are available for entry in:

  • October 2026
  • February 2027
  • July 2027

Applicants should ensure that their PhD offer from Loughborough University aligns with one of these available entry points.

Eligibility Criteria

To be eligible for the Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships, applicants must meet several important conditions.

Applicants must:

  1. Hold an offer for a PhD programme at Loughborough University

    Applicants must already have received an offer of study for a postgraduate research programme at Loughborough University. This offer may be either:

    • Conditional
    • Unconditional

    Scholarship applications will not be considered unless the applicant is holding an offer at the time of submitting the scholarship application.

  2. Apply for a postgraduate research programme first

    Applicants cannot apply for the scholarship before applying for and receiving an offer from Loughborough University. The scholarship application must come after the PhD offer has been issued.

  3. Show evidence of exceptional academic achievement

    Applicants must demonstrate strong academic ability. However, the University considers academic achievement in relation to the applicant’s circumstances. This is important because many at-risk scholars may have experienced serious disruption to their education.

    The University is not only looking at achievement in ideal conditions. It is also considering achievement relative to the barriers, instability, or disruption the applicant has faced.

  4. Be considered an at-risk scholar

    The scholarship is designed specifically for people whose safety, education, or life chances have been affected by conflict, persecution, displacement, or similar circumstances.

Who Is Considered an At-Risk Scholar?

Loughborough University explains that it is difficult to provide one fixed definition of an at-risk scholar. However, the University uses a framework to assess whether an applicant’s circumstances meet the purpose of the Sanctuary Scholarship scheme.

Applicants may be considered at risk if their situation involves one or more of the following:

  1. Living in a region affected by conflict or war

    Applicants may qualify if their home is located in a region where conflict or war places their life, or the lives of their family members, in danger.

  2. Risk of forced displacement

    Applicants may be considered at risk if they could be forcibly displaced due to persecution or dangerous conditions.

  3. Risk of imprisonment, injury, death, or persecution

    Applicants may qualify if they or their family face imminent danger, including the risk of imprisonment, injury, death, or persecution.

  4. Danger if required to return home

    Applicants who are currently in the UK may also qualify if returning to their home country would place their life or safety at much greater risk.

This framework allows the University to assess applicants whose circumstances may vary widely but who share a common need for protection, support, and access to education.

How to Apply for the Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships

The scholarship application process is an open competition. This means eligible applicants will be assessed competitively, and awards will be made based on the University’s selection process.

Applicants must follow the correct order when applying.

Step 1: Apply for a PhD programme at Loughborough University

Before applying for the Sanctuary Scholarship, applicants must first apply for a postgraduate research programme at Loughborough University.

Step 2: Receive a conditional or unconditional offer

Applicants must wait until they have received an official offer letter from Loughborough University. This offer can be conditional or unconditional.

Step 3: Apply for the scholarship

Once an applicant has received an offer, they can apply for the Sanctuary Scholarship using the online application form provided by the University.

The application link was referenced as “Apply now” in the provided information, but no direct URL was included here.

Step 4: Complete all parts of the application form

Applicants must ensure that every part of the scholarship application form is completed in full. Incomplete applications may not be considered.

Step 5: Wait for confirmation and outcome

After submitting the application, applicants should receive a confirmation email stating that the application has been received.

All applicants will be notified of the outcome by email.

Selection Process

Applications will first be shortlisted. After the shortlisting stage, the final decision on the scholarship awards will be made by a selection panel at Loughborough University.

If an applicant is shortlisted, the University will provide further information about the next steps in the process.

Applicants who receive an award while holding a conditional PhD offer should note that the scholarship will only be confirmed if they meet all conditions attached to their offer.

Important Notes for Applicants

Applicants should carefully read the important conditions attached to the scholarship before submitting their application.

Key points include:

  1. You must already hold a PhD offer

    Scholarship applications will not be considered unless the applicant is already holding a conditional or unconditional offer from Loughborough University.

  2. Do not apply before receiving your offer letter

    Applicants should not submit a scholarship application until they have received their offer letter.

  3. Incomplete applications may not be considered

    All sections of the application form must be completed in full.

  4. Late applications will not be accepted

    Applications received after the closing date will not be considered.

  5. Conditional offers must be fully met

    If a scholarship is awarded to an applicant with a conditional offer, the award will depend on the applicant meeting those conditions in full.

  6. Travel and visa costs are not covered

    Applicants must make separate arrangements to cover travel and visa-related expenses where required.

Part-Time PhD Study Conditions

The scholarship may consider applicants who wish to study part-time in the UK, but there are important restrictions.

If an applicant is currently outside the UK and wishes to study part-time, Loughborough University will not be able to sponsor them in the UK on a student visa.

For applicants already based in the UK who wish to be considered for a part-time PhD scholarship, additional conditions apply.

Applicants in full-time employment will not be eligible for a part-time scholarship. However, applicants may be considered if they:

  • Work part-time
  • Have caring responsibilities
  • Have other requirements that make part-time study necessary

This means part-time awards are possible, but they are not automatic and will depend on the applicant’s personal circumstances.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships is:

Monday 22 June 2026

Applications submitted after this date will not be considered.

Why This Scholarship Matters

The Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships provide more than financial support. They offer an opportunity for at-risk scholars to continue their education, rebuild academic pathways, and contribute to research despite the serious challenges they may have faced.

For individuals whose education has been interrupted by conflict, persecution, or displacement, access to doctoral study can be life-changing. A PhD can open doors to academic careers, research leadership, policy influence, professional advancement, and long-term personal stability.

The scholarship also strengthens the role of universities as institutions of protection, opportunity, and intellectual freedom. By supporting at-risk scholars, Loughborough University helps preserve academic talent that might otherwise be lost due to circumstances beyond the applicant’s control.

Final Reminder

The Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships are open to eligible PhD applicants who already hold an offer from Loughborough University and who can demonstrate that they are at risk due to conflict, persecution, displacement, or serious threats to safety.

Applicants should first secure a PhD offer, prepare a complete scholarship application, and submit it before the deadline of Monday 22 June 2026.

APPLY HERE.

For more information about this opportunity click here.

Discover more scholarship opportunities on OFY here.

The post Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships 2026/2027: Fully Funded PhD Scholarship for At-Risk Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: NASA Fall 2026 Science Communications Internship at Goddard Space Flight Center: A Comprehensive Guide for Aspiring Communicators
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
Introduction The NASA Fall 2026 Science Communications Internship at the Goddard Space Flight Center (GSFC) in Greenbelt, Maryland, offers an extraordinary opportunity for undergraduate and graduate students to gain hands-on experience in multimedia storytelling, public engagement, and internal communications. This in-person internship is designed for students who are passionate about science communication, journalism, media production, […]

The post NASA Fall 2026 Science Communications Internship at Goddard Space Flight Center: A Comprehensive Guide for Aspiring Communicators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Introduction

The NASA Fall 2026 Science Communications Internship at the Goddard Space Flight Center (GSFC) in Greenbelt, Maryland, offers an extraordinary opportunity for undergraduate and graduate students to gain hands-on experience in multimedia storytelling, public engagement, and internal communications. This in-person internship is designed for students who are passionate about science communication, journalism, media production, public relations, design, and related fields. With access to NASA’s most groundbreaking missions and communication teams, interns will immerse themselves in real-world projects that shape how the world understands space, Earth science, and technological innovation.

This article provides a detailed overview of the internship, including responsibilities, focus areas, eligibility, application tips, and key dates. It is crafted to help students understand the full scope of the opportunity and prepare a strong application.

About the NASA Science Communications Internship

NASA internships are immersive educational experiences that allow students to participate in NASA-related research, operations, and communications. This particular internship focuses on multimedia, social media, public engagement, and internal communications within NASA’s bustling communications department.

Interns will collaborate with expert writers, multimedia producers, public affairs officers, and experience designers to create compelling content that highlights NASA’s most exciting missions. These include:

  • The Hubble Space Telescope
  • The James Webb Space Telescope
  • The Roman Space Telescope
  • Artemis lunar exploration missions
  • Earth observation satellites
  • Solar observation missions

Interns will help translate complex scientific discoveries into engaging stories for diverse audiences.

Internship Focus Areas

This posting covers two primary roles. Applicants may indicate a preference for one or both.

1. Multimedia Newsroom

Interns in this track will contribute to high-impact communication campaigns by creating:

  • Web features
  • Videos
  • Media interviews
  • Social media content
  • Livestreams
  • Digital storytelling assets

Students may specialize in writing, video production, or digital/social media.

2. Public Engagement

This track focuses on outreach and internal engagement, including:

  • Public outreach campaigns
  • Event planning
  • Tour organization
  • Workforce engagement
  • Graphic and display design
  • Photography

Interns may choose to focus on tours, graphics, photography, or internal communications.

Key Responsibilities

Interns will engage in a wide range of creative and strategic tasks, including:

  • Writing
  • Designing
  • Hosting
  • Engaging with audiences
  • Leading and collaborating
  • Teaching and presenting
  • Creating multimedia content
  • Researching and analyzing
  • Captioning and shooting media
  • Updating digital platforms
  • Networking and managing communications
  • Tracking and monitoring engagement
  • Building and blending creative assets
  • Exploring new storytelling approaches

Desired Attributes

NASA seeks students who demonstrate:

  • Unbounded curiosity
  • A creative spirit
  • A passion for storytelling
  • Interest or experience in science
  • Strong communication skills
  • Ability to work collaboratively
  • Initiative and adaptability

Some of NASA’s most successful interns have been scientists with communication skills or communicators with a strong interest in science.

Work Schedule and Format

  • Full-time commitment: Approximately 40 hours per week
  • Schedule: Monday through Friday, regular working hours
  • Flexibility: Minor adjustments for mid-day classes may be possible
  • Format: In-person at Goddard Space Flight Center (GSFC), Greenbelt, Maryland

NASA emphasizes that the internship is best performed on-site due to the hands-on nature of the work.

Application Tips

To strengthen your application, NASA recommends:

  • Including a personal statement outlining your internship goals
  • Highlighting relevant coursework, internships, and work experience
  • Providing links to work samples (writing, video, design, photography, etc.)
  • Explaining how the internship aligns with your long-term career aspirations

Key Dates

  • Registration Start Date: March 16, 2026
  • Registration End Date: May 22, 2026
  • Internship Session: Fall 2026

Applicants must submit all materials before the registration deadline.

Location and Eligibility Requirements

  • Location: Goddard Space Flight Center (GSFC), Greenbelt, Maryland
  • Citizenship Requirement: U.S. citizens only
  • Eligible Academic Levels:
    • Undergraduate Freshman
    • Undergraduate Sophomore
    • Undergraduate Junior
    • Undergraduate Senior
    • Graduate Master’s students

Preferred Majors

NASA welcomes students from a wide range of communication, media, and design-related fields, including:

  • Journalism
  • Communications
  • Media studies
  • Public relations
  • Advertising
  • Strategic communications
  • Marketing
  • Graphic design
  • Photography
  • Audiovisual communications
  • Digital communication
  • Publishing
  • Radio, television, and digital media
  • Visual and performing arts
To apply and get more information about this opportunity visit here
Discover more  opportunities here

 

The post NASA Fall 2026 Science Communications Internship at Goddard Space Flight Center: A Comprehensive Guide for Aspiring Communicators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AfricaNenda SIIPS 2026 Report Cover Photo Competition: Win USD 500 for Your African Visual Storytelling
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The AfricaNenda Foundation has officially opened submissions for creatives, photographers, and visual artists to contribute the official cover design for the highly anticipated State of Inclusive Instant Payment Systems in Africa (SIIPS) 2026 Report. This exciting Request for Proposals (RFP) presents a unique opportunity for African visual storytellers to showcase their creativity, celebrate Africa’s diversity, […]

The post AfricaNenda SIIPS 2026 Report Cover Photo Competition: Win USD 500 for Your African Visual Storytelling appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The AfricaNenda Foundation has officially opened submissions for creatives, photographers, and visual artists to contribute the official cover design for the highly anticipated State of Inclusive Instant Payment Systems in Africa (SIIPS) 2026 Report. This exciting Request for Proposals (RFP) presents a unique opportunity for African visual storytellers to showcase their creativity, celebrate Africa’s diversity, and gain international visibility while competing for a USD 500 prize.

The selected cover image or concept will become the visual identity of the SIIPS 2026 Report, a publication that highlights the progress, innovation, and future of inclusive instant payment systems across Africa. The initiative aims to visually communicate Africa’s digital transformation, entrepreneurship ecosystem, financial inclusion efforts, and regional connectivity through authentic African imagery.

Applicants are encouraged to review previous SIIPS reports to better understand the storytelling style and visual direction expected for the 2026 edition.

About the SIIPS 2026 Report Cover Opportunity

The SIIPS report is recognized as an important publication documenting Africa’s evolving instant payment systems landscape. By contributing to the official report cover, photographers and creatives have an opportunity to position their work before policymakers, financial institutions, development organizations, fintech stakeholders, and audiences across Africa and beyond.

AfricaNenda is specifically looking for visually compelling and professional concepts that reflect:

  • Africa’s map and continental identity
  • Authentic African faces and communities
  • Africa’s cultural richness and regional diversity
  • Innovation and financial inclusion
  • Digital transactions and connectivity
  • Trade and entrepreneurship
  • Movement, networks, and human connection
  • A modern and professional aesthetic suitable for a continental report

The organization welcomes creative interpretations that combine storytelling, visual symbolism, and originality while remaining aligned with the report’s professional and policy-oriented audience.

Prize for the Selected Cover Design

The selected submission will receive:

  1. USD 500 cash prize
  2. Recognition through inclusion in the SIIPS 2026 Report
  3. Exposure to international development and financial inclusion stakeholders
  4. An opportunity to contribute to a major pan-African publication

Selected contributors will later be required to submit original high-resolution non-watermarked files and sign a simple licensing agreement before payment is processed.

Submission Requirements for Applicants

Applicants must carefully follow all submission instructions to ensure eligibility.

What to Include in Your Submission ZIP Folder

All submission materials must be compiled into a single ZIP folder containing:

  • Watermarked preview images in JPEG or PNG format
  • Original photographs only
  • A submission sample document in text, Word, or PDF format containing:
    • Proposed cover
    • Photographer’s full name
    • Country where each image was taken
    • Image captions or descriptions where possible
    • Contact information including:
      • Email address
      • Phone number or WhatsApp number with country code

Important Rules and Conditions

AfricaNenda has outlined strict originality and ownership requirements for all entries.

Key Conditions Include:

  • AI-generated images are strictly prohibited
  • All submissions must consist of original photographs taken by the entrant
  • Submitted images must not have previously won or been accepted in photography competitions
  • Photographers must own full rights to all submitted work
  • Images featuring identifiable individuals must include signed consent or release forms

Failure to meet any of these conditions may lead to disqualification.

Why This Opportunity Matters for African Creatives

This competition is more than just a photography submission call. It is an opportunity to visually shape conversations around financial inclusion and Africa’s digital economy.

The SIIPS report reaches a broad network of:

  • Financial institutions
  • Policymakers
  • Fintech companies
  • Development organizations
  • Investors
  • Researchers
  • Technology and payment ecosystem stakeholders

Having your work featured on the report cover can significantly boost professional visibility and strengthen your creative portfolio.

Deadline for Applications

The deadline for submissions is:

15 June 2026

Applicants are encouraged to submit early to avoid technical issues or last-minute delays.

How to Submit Your Entry

All submissions must be sent via email to: communications@africanenda.org

Applicants are advised to carefully review all requirements before submission and ensure that all materials are complete, professional, and properly organized.

The SIIPS 2026 cover competition offers African creatives a rare opportunity to merge artistic storytelling with Africa’s digital transformation narrative while gaining continental recognition for their work.

For more information about this opportunity visit here.

Discover more competition opportunities here.

The post AfricaNenda SIIPS 2026 Report Cover Photo Competition: Win USD 500 for Your African Visual Storytelling appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: NNC Foundation Programs 2026: Scholarships, Community Grants, Literacy Support & Immigration Fee Assistance Opportunities in the United States
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The NNC Foundation (NamKil & Noul Chung Foundation) is a registered 501(c)(3) tax-exempt nonprofit organization in the United States dedicated to transforming lives through education, literacy development, and targeted financial assistance programs. Through its structured initiatives, the foundation supports students, nonprofit organizations, and individuals facing immigration-related financial barriers. In 2026, the NNC Foundation continues to […]

The post NNC Foundation Programs 2026: Scholarships, Community Grants, Literacy Support & Immigration Fee Assistance Opportunities in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The NNC Foundation (NamKil & Noul Chung Foundation) is a registered 501(c)(3) tax-exempt nonprofit organization in the United States dedicated to transforming lives through education, literacy development, and targeted financial assistance programs. Through its structured initiatives, the foundation supports students, nonprofit organizations, and individuals facing immigration-related financial barriers.

In 2026, the NNC Foundation continues to offer four active programs designed to promote educational equity, strengthen communities, and support vulnerable populations across the United States.

These programs include scholarships for students, literacy and library development initiatives, nonprofit community partnership grants, and immigration filing fee support for employment-based applicants.

Overview of NNC Foundation Mission and Impact

The mission of the NNC Foundation is centered on empowering individuals and communities through:

  • Access to education and academic opportunity
  • Promotion of literacy and lifelong learning
  • Financial support for underserved populations
  • Strengthening nonprofit-community partnerships
  • Reducing barriers in immigration processes for eligible applicants

The foundation works with students, nonprofit organizations, and individuals in need, focusing on long-term social impact and sustainable development.

Active Programs Offered by NNC Foundation (2026)

The NNC Foundation currently runs four major programs, each targeting a specific area of social and educational development.

1. Global Domestic Scholarship Program

The Global Domestic Scholarship Program is one of the foundation’s flagship initiatives designed to support academically strong students from financially challenged backgrounds.

Program Objective

This scholarship aims to reward students who demonstrate:

  • Academic excellence
  • Strong community engagement
  • Leadership potential
  • Financial need

Scholarship Value

  • High School Students: $2,000 award
  • Undergraduate & Graduate Students: $3,000 award

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet all of the following conditions:

  • Must be a U.S. citizen, permanent resident, or have valid migratory status
  • Must be currently enrolled in an accredited institution in the United States
  • Must maintain a minimum GPA of 3.0 on a 4.0 scale
  • Must demonstrate financial need and academic achievement

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit:

  • Essay (PDF format)
  • Most recent tax return (Form 1040)
  • Academic transcript
  • Recommendation letter

Contact

Help@nncfoundation.org

2. Literacy & Library Programs

The Literacy & Library Programs are community-focused initiatives aimed at strengthening education through reading and knowledge development.

Program Purpose

This initiative focuses on:

  • Enhancing language comprehension skills
  • Promoting reading culture among children and youth
  • Supporting local libraries and educational infrastructure
  • Encouraging lifelong learning habits

Key Impact Areas

  • Local communities across the United States
  • International educational outreach partnerships
  • School-based and public library support systems

Program Focus

  • Literacy education development
  • Library operations support
  • Reading and comprehension programs
  • Community learning enhancement

Contact

Help@nncfoundation.org

3. NNC Community Partnership Program

The Community Partnership Program provides funding and collaboration opportunities for nonprofit organizations working at the grassroots level.

Program Objective

This grant program is designed to support organizations that address:

  • Education inequality
  • Literacy improvement
  • Community health development
  • Poverty reduction
  • Social innovation initiatives

Grant Focus

  • Support for underserved and low-income populations
  • Capacity building for nonprofit organizations
  • Sustainable community development projects

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Be a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization
  • Have a mission aligned with education, health, or community development
  • Demonstrate at least 1–2 years of active community service experience

Application Status

  • Currently accepting applications

Contact

Help@nncfoundation.org

4. Immigration Filing Fee Support Grant   

The Immigration Filing Fee Support Grant provides critical financial assistance to individuals navigating employment-based immigration processes in the United States.

Program Objective

This grant is designed to reduce financial barriers by covering USCIS filing fees for eligible applicants.

Key Support Features

  • Direct payment of filing fees to USCIS on behalf of applicants
  • Financial assistance for employment-based immigration petitions
  • One-time award per beneficiary

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be a beneficiary of an employment-based immigration petition
  • Be currently in the immigration petition process
  • Demonstrate financial need
  • Provide official documentation of the petition and USCIS fee instructions

Important Notes

  • Grant payment is issued directly to USCIS
  • Only main applicants are eligible
  • Each applicant may receive only one award

Contact

Help@nncfoundation.org

About the NNC Foundation

The NamKil & Noul Chung Foundation is a U.S.-based nonprofit organization dedicated to advancing educational opportunity, community empowerment, and immigrant support services.

Organization Details

  • Status: 501(c)(3) Tax-Exempt Organization
  • Address: PO Box #3301, Duluth, GA 30096, United States
  • Phone: (470) 282-1643
  • Email: Help@nncfoundation.org

Why These Programs Matter

The NNC Foundation programs collectively aim to:

  • Reduce educational inequality
  • Support students from low-income families
  • Strengthen literacy and learning infrastructure
  • Empower nonprofit organizations
  • Provide financial relief to immigration applicants

These initiatives play a crucial role in building stronger communities and supporting long-term human development goals.

Conclusion

The NNC Foundation 2026 programs represent a strong commitment to education, community development, and immigrant support within the United States. Whether you are a student seeking financial aid, a nonprofit organization looking for funding, or an individual navigating immigration costs, these programs provide meaningful opportunities for assistance and growth.

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here  
  • More global Scholarship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post NNC Foundation Programs 2026: Scholarships, Community Grants, Literacy Support & Immigration Fee Assistance Opportunities in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: IOM Global Shared Services Centre (GSSC) Philippines Recruitment 2026: Latest Job Vacancies, Roles, Deadlines, and How to Apply
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The International Organization for Migration (IOM) continues to expand its global humanitarian and administrative operations through its Global Shared Services Centre (GSSC), also known as the Manila Administrative Centre (MAC). As part of its worldwide mission to support migration governance and humanitarian assistance, IOM is currently recruiting for multiple positions across administration, human resources, supply […]

The post IOM Global Shared Services Centre (GSSC) Philippines Recruitment 2026: Latest Job Vacancies, Roles, Deadlines, and How to Apply appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Organization for Migration (IOM) continues to expand its global humanitarian and administrative operations through its Global Shared Services Centre (GSSC), also known as the Manila Administrative Centre (MAC). As part of its worldwide mission to support migration governance and humanitarian assistance, IOM is currently recruiting for multiple positions across administration, human resources, supply chain, healthcare, data analysis, payroll, and consultancy roles.

All positions advertised under this recruitment cycle are based in the Philippines, specifically within the Manila Global Administrative Centre, a strategic hub supporting IOM operations worldwide.

About the International Organization for Migration (IOM)

The International Organization for Migration (IOM) is a leading intergovernmental organization working in the field of migration management. It operates globally to support migrants, governments, and communities through humanitarian response, development programs, and policy support.

As part of the United Nations migration system, IOM emphasizes:

  • Efficiency and high professional standards
  • Integrity and accountability in humanitarian work
  • Diversity and inclusion across its global workforce
  • Equitable geographical representation in staffing
  • Multicultural collaboration in all duty stations

Working at IOM means contributing directly to life-saving operations, global migration governance, and humanitarian coordination across countries.

Overview of IOM Global Shared Services Centre (GSSC) – Manila

The Manila-based Administrative Centre serves as a global operational hub that supports:

  • Human resources management
  • Supply chain and logistics coordination
  • Medical and health data services
  • Payroll and financial administration
  • IT, data analysis, and digital transformation
  • Recruitment and talent acquisition

This center plays a key role in ensuring that IOM field missions operate efficiently across continents.

Latest Job Vacancies at IOM GSSC (2026)

Below are the currently open positions, including deadlines and grades:

1. Asset and Inventory Assistant (G-4)

  • Code: VN 047/2026
  • Deadline: 22 May 2026
  • Role: Manage inventory systems, asset tracking, and logistics documentation.

2. HR Associate (Ungraded)

  • Code: CFA 005/2026
  • Deadline: 22 May 2026
  • Role: Support HR processes, recruitment administration, and employee records.

3. National Medical Officer (Ungraded)

  • Code: CFA 006/2026
  • Deadline: 24 May 2026
  • Role: Provide medical expertise for staff health and humanitarian operations.

4. Human Resources Intern (Open to Fresh Graduates)

  • Code: INT 007/2026
  • Deadline: 29 May 2026
  • Role: Entry-level HR support and administrative learning opportunity.

5. Supply Chain Associate (Master Data Management) (G-5)

  • Code: VN 048/2026
  • Deadline: 29 May 2026
  • Role: Manage supply chain data systems and procurement support.

6. National Teleradiology Data Analysis Officer (NO-A)

  • Code: VN 049/2026
  • Deadline: 29 May 2026
  • Role: Analyze medical imaging data and support remote diagnostics systems.

7. Senior Payroll Administrator (Ungraded)

  • Code: CFA 007-008/2026
  • Deadline: 04 June 2026
  • Role: Oversee payroll systems, salary processing, and financial compliance.

8. National HR Officer (Talent Acquisition Regional Focal) (NO-B)

  • Code: VN 050/2026
  • Deadline: 04 June 2026
  • Role: Lead recruitment strategies and regional talent acquisition processes.

9. SEO Specialist (G-6)

  • Code: VN 031/2026
  • Deadline: 04 June 2026
  • Role: Optimize digital visibility, web content strategy, and search engine performance.

10. Radiologist (Teleradiology) Consultant

  • Code: CON 011-018/2026
  • Deadline: 05 June 2026
  • Role: Provide expert radiology consultation through remote diagnostic systems.

Key Features of Working with IOM GSSC

Working within the Manila Administrative Centre offers:

  • International exposure in a UN-related environment
  • Multidisciplinary career development opportunities
  • Competitive and structured global salary scales
  • Experience in humanitarian and migration-focused operations
  • Multicultural and inclusive workplace culture
  • Opportunities for career progression within the UN system

How to Apply

Interested candidates are encouraged to apply directly through the official IOM careers platform by submitting:

  • Updated CV/resume
  • Cover letter tailored to the position
  • Academic certificates and relevant professional documents
  • Proof of experience (where required)

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Why This Opportunity Matters

These vacancies represent a significant opportunity to:

  • Join a UN-affiliated organization
  • Contribute to global humanitarian and migration governance
  • Work in a structured international administrative environment
  • Build a career in HR, logistics, healthcare, data analysis, or digital systems

Conclusion

The IOM GSSC Manila recruitment drive for 2026 offers diverse career pathways for professionals and fresh graduates alike. With roles ranging from internships to senior consultancy positions, this is a key opportunity for individuals seeking international careers within the United Nations system.

Apply here 

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here 
  • More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post IOM Global Shared Services Centre (GSSC) Philippines Recruitment 2026: Latest Job Vacancies, Roles, Deadlines, and How to Apply appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: World Vision Brussels Careers 2026: Humanitarian Advocacy & Policy Specialist and Junior Communication & Policy Officer Opportunities in Belgium
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
World Vision International has announced exciting career opportunities within its European Union Representation Office (EUREP) in Brussels, Belgium. These openings provide professionals and young graduates with the opportunity to contribute to global humanitarian advocacy, European Union policy engagement, child-focused development work, and international communications. The organization is currently recruiting for two important positions: Humanitarian Advocacy […]

The post World Vision Brussels Careers 2026: Humanitarian Advocacy & Policy Specialist and Junior Communication & Policy Officer Opportunities in Belgium appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
World Vision International has announced exciting career opportunities within its European Union Representation Office (EUREP) in Brussels, Belgium. These openings provide professionals and young graduates with the opportunity to contribute to global humanitarian advocacy, European Union policy engagement, child-focused development work, and international communications.

The organization is currently recruiting for two important positions:

  1. Humanitarian Advocacy & Policy Specialist
  2. Junior Communication & Policy Officer (Trainee)

Both opportunities are based in Brussels and offer candidates the chance to work with one of the world’s largest humanitarian and development organizations while engaging directly with European Union institutions, advocacy networks, and global policy initiatives.

These positions are ideal for individuals passionate about humanitarian affairs, development cooperation, communications, public policy, advocacy, children’s rights, and international relations.

About World Vision

World Vision International is a global Christian humanitarian, development, and advocacy organization dedicated to improving the lives of vulnerable children, families, and communities worldwide.

Operating in nearly 100 countries with more than 31,000 staff members, World Vision works across areas such as:

  • Child protection
  • Emergency response
  • Humanitarian assistance
  • Poverty reduction
  • Education
  • Health and nutrition
  • Advocacy and policy engagement
  • Community resilience
  • Development programming

The organization collaborates with governments, institutions, NGOs, donors, and local communities to create sustainable and impactful solutions to global challenges affecting children and vulnerable populations.

About the World Vision EU Representation Office (EUREP)

The Brussels-based EU Representation Office supports World Vision’s engagement with European Union institutions and stakeholders.

The office focuses on two major strategic priorities:

1. Influencing EU Policies and Decision-Making

The office works to ensure that European Union policies, funding programs, and political decisions positively impact vulnerable children and communities around the world.

This involves:

  • Policy advocacy
  • Humanitarian diplomacy
  • Stakeholder engagement
  • Campaign coordination
  • Strategic communication
  • Institutional partnerships

2. Facilitating Access to EU Funding Opportunities

The office also supports World Vision teams globally in accessing EU development and humanitarian funding opportunities.

As one of the largest international donors, the European Union plays a critical role in financing global humanitarian and development initiatives. World Vision’s Brussels office helps connect field offices and programs with these funding mechanisms.

Position 1: Humanitarian Advocacy & Policy Specialist

Job Location

Brussels, Belgium

Employment Type

Permanent full-time contract

Department

Humanitarian Team

Start Date

As soon as possible (ASAP)

Overview of the Humanitarian Advocacy & Policy Specialist Role

The Humanitarian Advocacy & Policy Specialist will play a central role in supporting World Vision’s humanitarian engagement with European Union institutions.

The selected candidate will contribute to:

  • EU humanitarian advocacy strategies
  • Policy analysis
  • Institutional engagement
